description:
Goal: make source forms define modules, functions, macros, types, protocols, imports, aliases, and requires through compiler2-owned services that publish existing fact families.

Signal:
- literal source that does not use macros still publishes the same downstream facts as today: `ModuleIndexed`, `CodeScoped`, `ModuleDefined`, `FunctionDefined`, `TypeDefined` waits, `ProtocolDispatch`, and function type-reference wait sets
- `def`, `defmodule`, `defmacro`, `@type`, `@spec`, `defprotocol`, and `defimpl` are handled through one source-production service boundary
- definition facts include enough source identity that changing a produced body or macro expansion bumps the owning revision and wakes lower/contract/semantic jobs correctly
- downstream body/dispatch/semantic/artifact jobs do not learn whether a definition was literal source or produced source

Change:
- replace direct `define_scope` over `Rc<Item>` with source-production services over owned compiler2 source AST
- centralize function/module/type/protocol definition publication so literal source and macro-produced source use the same path
- preserve existing type-reference recording and contract/type-definition waits at the service boundary
- keep protocol type facts and dispatch facts as facts, without folding protocol-domain cleanup from `fz-rh2.12.5` into this ticket

Why:
- Elixir-style `def` works because the compiler remains the authority; macros produce or request source definitions, but compiler services store them
- this is the seam that keeps downstream compiler2 structurally unchanged
title: Compiler2 compiler services: publish facts from quoted source forms
